title: The Jersey Stack — Ep 1: The Transistor (DRAFT v4)
description:
🎬 DRAFT v4 — for review only, not for public sharing.

New Jersey invented the transistor. A 20-mile stretch of NJ holds the birthplace of the thing that runs every device in your pocket, and a monument to itself just outside the Severance set. Episode 1 of The Jersey Stack follows the story from Bell Labs Murray Hill in 1947 through the Traitorous Eight walkout in 1957 — and ends back at Fort Monmouth, where Netflix is rebuilding the ground that built the hardware.

What changed in v4:
• Narration-budget enforcement — every image on screen ≥10s (floor), fixing Scene 2 and other overpacked scenes where images were flashing too fast for the narration
• Scene 1 drone now plays at native 7s (video-driven) — no forced sync with 2.4s narration
• Storyboard app is now the single source of truth — pacing computed per-scene from narration WAV durations
• Render manifest (output/render-manifest-v4.json) logs any images the budget pass dropped

Known issues still in this cut:
• Scene 29 closing cutaway montage still deferred (drone hold remains)
• Some AI-generated text (S20 newspaper, S26 logo sequence) may have rendering artifacts
• Pacing trim review still pending on longer scenes

Chapters:
0:00 Drone Reveal & Severance Hook
1:05 Title Card
1:20 Bell Labs Murray Hill
3:25 The Vacuum Tube Problem
5:10 The Inventors
7:20 The Demo (Dec 23, 1947)
9:55 Shockley Chicago & the Junction
11:45 The June 1948 Press Conference
12:30 Fort Monmouth & InfoAge
13:40 Nobel & the Consent Decree
14:05 Shockley Semi & the Walkout
14:30 The Count-Up
14:50 Back to the Drone — Netflix Fort Monmouth

---
Part of The Jersey Stack — a five-episode series on how New Jersey invented the modern computer.
